successCriteria.description: Create `src/modules/uptime/uptime.routes.ts` as a Fastify plugin that registers a GET /uptime route delegating to `UptimeService.getUptime()`, following the existing module pattern (import service interface + implementation from the module's own barrel).
`src/app.ts` must register `uptimeRoutes` without import or runtime errors â€” the application must start successfully and respond to GET /uptime.
`src/modules/uptime/index.ts` must correctly re-export `UptimeStatus`, `IUptimeService`, `UptimeService`, and `uptimeRoutes` â€” all from files that actually exist.
All existing Jest tests (`tests/unit/shared/base.repository.test.ts`, `tests/unit/shared/types.test.ts`) must continue to pass with no regressions.
`npm run build` (or `tsc --noEmit`) must succeed with zero type errors after the fix.

constraints: The new `uptime.routes.ts` must follow the existing module pattern: import the service interface from the module barrel (`index.ts`), instantiate the service, and export a Fastify plugin function.
No changes to `src/shared/` files â€” the base repository, types, and DB connection are not in scope for this fix.
The `status` module (`src/modules/status/`) is out of scope â€” do not wire it into `app.ts`, do not add routes for it, and do not delete it.
All database access must go through the repository pattern (GP-001). The uptime route does not touch the database, so this is a non-issue for this fix, but must not introduce any direct DB calls.
outOfScope: Wiring the orphaned `status` module (`src/modules/status/`) into `app.ts` â€” it remains as-is.
Adding authentication, RBAC middleware, or any auth infrastructure â€” that is deferred to Phase 9.
Adding audit logging or notification wiring to the uptime route â€” uptime is a read-only health-check endpoint, not a state-changing operation.
Any changes to `src/shared/` (types, base repository, DB connection) â€” those are stable and not implicated in this fix.

ambiguities.description: Should `app.ts` import `uptimeRoutes` from the module barrel (`./modules/uptime`) following the architecture rule that modules import only from index.ts, or keep the existing direct import from `./modules/uptime/uptime.routes`? The architecture says "Modules import from each other ONLY through their declared public entry point (index.ts)" but `app.ts` is the application root, not a peer module.
options: Keep direct import from ./modules/uptime/uptime.routes (match existing app.ts pattern)
Change to barrel import from ./modules/uptime (follow architecture rule strictly)

ambiguities.description: Should the GET /uptime endpoint require authentication/RBAC? No auth middleware exists yet in the codebase (Phase 9 introduces RBAC). The uptime endpoint is a simple health-check-style route. Making it public now is reasonable, but if it should be protected, the fix would need to add auth infrastructure that isn't planned until Phase 9.
options: Leave GET /uptime as a public (unauthenticated) endpoint
Add a placeholder auth guard that always passes (defer real auth to Phase 9)

ambiguities.description: The `status` module (`src/modules/status/`) has a model, service interface, service implementation, and barrel â€” but no routes, no controller, and is not registered in `app.ts`. It appears to be dead/orphaned code. Should it be left as-is, wired up, or removed?
options: Leave the status module as-is (orphaned, not wired)
Wire the status module into app.ts with a GET /status route
